content: In today's fast-paced world, technology plays a crucial role in shaping how we live, work, and communicate. It's important to note that as we navigate the complexities of modern life, we must also consider the impact of digital transformation. Overall, embracing innovation is key to success in this ever-evolving landscape.
topic: Technology and digital transformation
explanation:
This text is classified as AI work because it uses broad statements, polished grammar, and predictable transition phrases without giving concrete examples, evidence, or a personal perspective. The paragraph sounds fluent, but its ideas remain general and could apply to almost any discussion about technology.

What to look for:
• Uses generic phrases such as 'fast-paced world'
• Relies on predictable transitions
• Contains broad claims without examples
• Maintains an evenly polished tone
• Lacks a distinctive personal voice

Source: The text was generated by AI.

content: Exercise offers numerous benefits for both physical and mental health. Firstly, it improves cardiovascular health. Secondly, it boosts mood through the release of endorphins. Thirdly, it enhances sleep quality. In conclusion, incorporating regular exercise into your daily routine is essential for overall well-being.
topic: Benefits of regular exercise
explanation:
This text is classified as AI work because it follows a highly structured list pattern using 'Firstly,' 'Secondly,' 'Thirdly,' and 'In conclusion.' The information is accurate-sounding but generic, with no supporting data, personal example, or unusual wording. Its mechanical organization makes the classification easy.

What to look for:
• Uses a formulaic numbered structure
• Ends with a predictable conclusion
• Provides common knowledge only
• Includes no evidence or source
• Has a uniform and impersonal tone

Source: The text was generated by AI.

content: Losing my grandmother was a significant event in my life. It taught me valuable lessons about the importance of family and cherishing the moments we have with loved ones. Grief is a natural part of the human experience, and it's important to allow oneself to process these emotions in a healthy way.
topic: Grief and the loss of a grandmother
explanation:
This text is classified as AI work because it discusses a deeply personal loss using abstract and generalized language. It does not include a specific memory, place, habit, quotation, or sensory detail connected to the grandmother. The paragraph quickly turns the experience into a universal life lesson, which is a common pattern in generic AI writing.

What to look for:
• Describes grief in abstract terms
• Includes no specific memory
• Moves quickly to a general life lesson
• Uses emotionally safe and polished wording
• Could apply to many different people

Source: The text was generated by AI.

content: She was my alarm clock every morning. She would peek her head through the door at 5 am and say, “Padmini, ley-amma. 5 ayindi.” (Padmini, wake up, it’s 5 am.) I’d respond promptly with “5 more minutes, Ammamma!” She’d say in her sweet voice, “Ok, ma.” She’d walk in exactly 5 minutes later to wake me up again. She did that as many times as I needed.
topic: A grandmother's morning wake-up routine
explanation:
This text is classified as human work because it contains a specific repeated family routine, direct dialogue, personal names, and untranslated cultural expressions. Details such as the exact time, the five-minute delay, and the grandmother's manner of speaking create an individual memory rather than a general description of family love.

What to look for:
• Contains specific names and family terms
• Includes natural dialogue
• Uses culturally specific language
• Describes an exact repeated routine
• Shows an individual and memorable voice

Source: Written by human - Pyapali, P. (2021, April 10). Tribute to Ammamma. Small Big Ideas.

content: like I said, not a lot of stability. but we had one thing that was stable in our tumultuous lives, our grandparents. something that I am so incredibly grateful for is the fact that we grew up in las vegas with two sets of grandparents within 20 min of us. we saw them weekly, during elementary school my yaya and papou watched us most days after school. and in middle school it was my grandma jo and papa. we had dinner with them most nights of the week. and it was lovely.
topic: Childhood stability provided by grandparents
explanation:
This text is classified as human work because it preserves an informal personal writing style, including lowercase sentence openings, conversational phrasing, and family-specific names. The writer recalls concrete locations, school periods, distances, routines, and different grandparents, producing a lived-in account with natural imperfections.

What to look for:
• Uses informal lowercase writing
• Names specific relatives
• Mentions Las Vegas and a 20-minute distance
• Connects memories to school periods
• Contains natural stylistic imperfections

Source: Written by human - Olivas, K. (2024, September 16). #51: i’ve lost my grandmother.

topic: Political misinformation about LGBTQ rights
explanation:
This image is classified as AI-generated because the hands, fingers and background faces contain unnatural details. The news logo also contains meaningless text. The overly staged scene, dramatic lighting and inconsistent visual elements are common signs of AI-generated content.

What to look for:
• Distorted hands and fingers
• Inconsistent background faces
• Meaningless news logo text
• Overly staged composition
• Unnatural crowd details

Source: This image was generated by AI

topic: False international relations announcement
explanation:
This image is classified as AI-generated because several signs and logos contain incorrect or meaningless text. The handshake, fingers, flags and background participants also show visual distortions that are commonly found in synthetic images.

What to look for:
• Incorrect organisation names
• Inconsistent logos and signs
• Unusual handshake details
• Distorted background faces
• Fabricated breaking-news design

Source: This image was generated by AI

topic: Fabricated sports corruption scandal
explanation:
This image is classified as AI-generated because it presents an exaggerated and unrealistic exchange of money. Several hands, faces and background details appear distorted, while the sensational layout combines multiple dramatic elements to imitate a news report.

What to look for:
• Unrealistic money exchange
• Distorted hands and faces
• Inconsistent small text
• Exaggerated emotional reactions
• Sensational collage-style layout

Source: This image was generated by AI

topic: Effects of trade tariffs on food prices
explanation:
This image is classified as human-produced because it appears to be a real television broadcast screenshot. The presenters, studio environment, lighting and body proportions look natural. The text and graphics are readable and consistently arranged.

What to look for:
• Natural facial features
• Realistic studio lighting
• Readable and consistent text
• Professional broadcast layout
• Accurate shadows and proportions

Source: This is real news from CNN television broadcast

topic: Political violence and public safety
explanation:
This image is classified as human-produced because the presenters show natural expressions, posture and interaction. The studio lighting, table reflections and broadcast graphics are physically and visually consistent.

What to look for:
• Natural presenter interaction
• Realistic body posture
• Consistent studio reflections
• Clear news graphics
• Professional broadcast arrangement

Source: This is real news from CNN television broadcast

topic: Street interview in a busy city
explanation:
This video is classified as AI work because it presents a highly polished street-interview scene that appears synthetic across time. The people remain unusually camera-ready in a crowded environment, while some background pedestrians, facial expressions, hand-to-microphone contact, and small clothing details may appear less consistent between moments. These temporal inconsistencies are common clues in AI-generated videos and are easier to notice when the clip is paused or replayed slowly.

What to look for:
• Background people may change or blend between frames
• Facial and mouth movements can look overly smooth
• Hand and microphone contact may appear slightly unnatural
• Small clothing details are not always temporally consistent
• The crowded scene looks unusually polished and staged

Source: The video was generated by AI

topic: Ancient battle and Trojan Horse scene
explanation:
This video is classified as AI work because the cinematic battle scene contains visual details that can shift as the camera moves. Background soldiers, armour, faces, fire, smoke, and parts of the wooden horse may lose consistency between frames. The main character's expressions are also unusually dramatic, and the entire event is composed like a short fantasy film rather than naturally captured historical footage.

What to look for:
• Background soldiers may morph or change appearance
• Armour and costume details are not fully consistent
• Fire and smoke move in an artificial-looking way
• The wooden horse contains unstable fine details
• Expressions and camera movement are highly cinematic

Source: The video was generated by AI

topic: An elderly sailor speaking at sea
explanation:
This video is classified as AI work because the sailor's face, mouth, hand, and pipe may not remain perfectly consistent throughout the clip. The pipe changes position while being handled, and the finger shapes and contact with the object can appear unnatural. The ocean and lighting are visually impressive, but the scene has a generated cinematic quality and limited natural camera imperfections.

What to look for:
• The pipe's shape or position may change
• Finger shapes and object contact can look unnatural
• Lip movement may not fully match natural speech
• Facial details may shift subtly between frames
• The sea scene looks unusually cinematic and controlled

Source: The video was generated by AI

topic: A detained person speaking from a police vehicle
explanation:
This video is classified as human work because it shows naturally recorded news footage of a person inside a police vehicle speaking toward people outside. The camera framing, sunlight, reflections, vehicle bars, facial movements, and partial obstructions remain physically consistent as the scene develops. The WSB-TV watermark and ordinary field-reporting quality also support its origin as human-recorded broadcast footage.

What to look for:
• Carries a consistent WSB-TV news watermark
• Natural outdoor lighting and vehicle reflections
• Stable car bars and surrounding geometry
• Realistic facial and head movements
• Ordinary field-camera framing and compression

Source: Real human-produced news footage carrying the WSB-TV watermark.

topic: A bull pushing and attacking an SUV
explanation:
This video is classified as human work because the bull, vehicle, road, shadows, and bystanders behave consistently throughout the event. The animal's weight and movement affect the SUV in a believable physical way, while the camera position and image quality resemble an ordinary recording made by a witness. The FailArmy watermark indicates that the clip was distributed as real user-recorded viral footage.

What to look for:
• Carries a consistent FailArmy watermark
• Bull anatomy remains stable during movement
• Vehicle movement follows believable physical force
• Shadows and road details remain consistent
• Looks like imperfect witness-recorded footage

Source: Real human-recorded viral footage carrying the FailArmy watermark.

topic: Professional workplace or job interview
explanation:
This video is classified as human work because it uses a natural shot-reverse-shot interview structure between two people. Their identities, clothing, hair, posture, eye direction, and facial movements remain consistent when the camera changes angles. The office lighting, depth of field, blinking, speech movements, and conversational reactions also resemble conventional live-action filming.

What to look for:
• Natural shot-reverse-shot editing
• Consistent faces, clothing, and hairstyles
• Realistic eye contact and conversational reactions
• Stable office background and lighting
• Natural blinking, posture, and facial movement

Source: Real - this video was taken by human.

content: I remember the first time I tried surfing. The water was cold, and the waves seemed intimidating at first. But after a few attempts, I started to get the hang of balancing on the board. It was a humbling experience, and it taught me that persistence pays off, even when things feel difficult in the beginning.
topic: Learning to surf
explanation:
This text is classified as AI work because it imitates a personal memory but gives only familiar, low-detail descriptions such as cold water and intimidating waves. The experience develops in a very neat sequence and ends with a clear moral about persistence. It sounds more personal than the easy examples, but it still lacks distinctive details such as a location, companion, mistake, or unexpected moment.

What to look for:
• Imitates a first-person memory
• Uses familiar rather than unique details
• Follows a neat beginning-to-lesson structure
• Ends with an explicit moral
• Provides no identifiable setting or people

Source: Generated by AI

content: Remote work has transformed the modern workplace in significant ways. On one hand, it offers flexibility and eliminates commute time, allowing employees to better manage their schedules. On the other hand, some argue it can lead to isolation and blur the boundaries between work and personal life. Ultimately, the effectiveness of remote work depends on individual preferences and company culture.
topic: Advantages and disadvantages of remote work
explanation:
This text is classified as AI work because it uses a standard balanced-discussion template: an opening claim, one advantage, one disadvantage, and a neutral conclusion. The paragraph is coherent and plausible, but it does not cite research, describe a real workplace, or take a distinctive position.

What to look for:
• Uses an 'on one hand/on the other hand' template
• Balances both sides mechanically
• Ends with a neutral conclusion
• Includes no concrete workplace example
• Has a polished but generic academic tone

Source: Generated by AI

content: Historians estimate that ancient trade routes connecting Southeast Asia to the Middle East date back over a thousand years, facilitating not just the exchange of goods like spices and textiles, but also ideas, religion, and technology. This cultural exchange played a foundational role in shaping the region's diverse identity today.
topic: Ancient trade routes and cultural exchange
explanation:
This text is classified as AI work because it adopts an authoritative historical tone while giving no named historian, route, period, archaeological evidence, or citation. The claim is plausible and smoothly summarized, making it harder than the easy examples, but its broad scope and unsupported attribution to 'historians' are warning signs.

What to look for:
• Uses vague attribution to 'historians'
• Provides no dates beyond a broad estimate
• Names no specific trade route
• Summarizes a complex history very smoothly
• Contains no citation or supporting evidence

Source: Generated by AI

content: When I started my first job, I thought confidence meant always having the answer. It took a few embarrassing mistakes before I realized that asking questions was often the faster way to learn.
topic: Learning confidence in a first job
explanation:
This text is classified as human work in the supplied dataset because it presents a compact reflection built around a change in the writer's own belief. The admission of embarrassing mistakes and the contrast between appearing confident and asking questions create a believable moment of self-correction. However, style alone cannot prove human authorship, and the supplied reference should be verified before publication.

What to look for:
• Shows a change in personal belief
• Admits embarrassing mistakes
• Expresses self-correction rather than a generic lesson
• Uses concise and natural reflection
• Includes a reference supplied by the dataset creator

Source: Written by human from What you’ll wish you’d known by Paul Graham.

content: I used to think happiness came from reaching the next milestone. Instead, every achievement simply created another goal. It took me years to appreciate ordinary days.
topic: Rethinking happiness and achievement
explanation:
This text is classified as human work in the supplied dataset because it expresses a personal shift developed over years and compresses that realization into a restrained reflection. The contrast between achievement and ordinary days gives it a focused viewpoint rather than a general list of advice. The attribution is broad, so it should be independently checked before being treated as a direct quotation.

What to look for:
• Describes a long-term change in perspective
• Uses a restrained reflective tone
• Builds around a clear personal contrast
• Avoids excessive explanation
• Has a broad reference that requires verification

Source: Written by human - Alain de Botton, The School of Life.

topic: Cryptocurrency market collapse
explanation:
This image is classified as AI work because it imitates a professional news broadcast but presents an extremely sensational claim in a highly dramatized way. The composition is unusually polished, the anchor’s shocked reaction looks exaggerated, and the chart appears designed to perfectly support the headline. The branding also looks generic rather than clearly tied to a real newsroom, which suggests a synthetic or AI-composed image.

What to look for:
• Sensational and unrealistic headline
• Overly dramatic presenter reaction
• Chart perfectly matches the narrative
• Broadcast design looks polished but generic
• Feels constructed to maximize shock value

Source: This image was generated by AI

topic: User data privacy and government surveillance allegations
explanation:
This image is classified as AI work because it combines several dramatic elements in a very deliberate and emotionally manipulative way. The inset graphic includes a large company logo, a 'USER DATA SOLD' stamp, and a 'TOP SECRET' government document, all arranged to force a specific conclusion. Although the image looks realistic at first glance, the message is oversimplified and visually staged, which is a common pattern in AI-generated misinformation content.

What to look for:
• Highly staged inset graphic
• Use of dramatic labels like 'USER DATA SOLD' and 'TOP SECRET'
• Visuals are designed to push one conclusion
• Lacks clear evidence or sourcing in the image
• Looks realistic but feels artificially constructed

Source: This image was generated by AI

topic: Regional independence and political unrest
explanation:
This image is classified as AI work because, although it appears realistic, the protest scene looks too conveniently aligned with the headline. The signs in the crowd strongly reinforce the exact message of the news banner, and the overall scene feels constructed to support the claim rather than document a real event naturally. The breaking-news layout is also simplified and attention-grabbing, which is typical of AI-generated political misinformation images.

What to look for:
• Protest signs align too perfectly with the headline
• Scene appears designed to support the claim
• Breaking-news layout is simplified and dramatic
• Crowd composition feels somewhat repetitive
• Looks believable at first but is likely synthetic

Source: This image was generated by AI

topic: Live discussion on international conflict and military action
explanation:
This image is classified as human work because it shows the characteristics of a real live television broadcast. The split-screen layout, studio anchor, remote guest, and main interview footage are presented in a natural and consistent way. Lighting, framing, and facial expressions all look realistic, and the ticker and lower-third graphics match the style of professional human-edited broadcast production.

What to look for:
• Natural split-screen live broadcast layout
• Realistic lighting and camera framing
• Consistent lower-third and market ticker
• Natural facial expressions and posture
• Looks like an authentic TV news screenshot

Source: This is real news from CNN television broadcast

topic: Political discussion about election-related claims
explanation:
This image is classified as human work because it appears to be taken from a real television interview or discussion program. The two speakers interact naturally, the studio background is coherent, and the on-screen captions are professionally arranged. The slight softness and compression in the image also resemble an actual video screenshot rather than a perfectly rendered synthetic image.

What to look for:
• Natural interaction between speakers
• Realistic studio environment
• Professional and coherent text layout
• Authentic live-video quality and compression
• Consistent broadcast presentation

Source: This is real news from CNN television broadcast

topic: Cleaning a tabletop in a dimly lit room
explanation:
This video is classified as AI work because the simple cleaning action contains subtle continuity problems across time. The transparent bottle, cap, fingers, and wiping hand do not always keep perfectly stable shapes while they move. The cloth or hand also appears to slide across the tabletop with unusually smooth contact, while highlights on the bottle and table change in a slightly artificial way. The very dark lighting helps hide these small defects, which makes the clip suitable for the medium level.

What to look for:
• The bottle and cap show slight shape changes during movement
• Finger shapes and grip are not fully stable
• The wiping motion appears unusually smooth
• Reflections on the bottle and tabletop shift inconsistently
• Low lighting hides many of the synthetic details

Source: This video was generated by AI

topic: A young man checking his phone beside takeaway food
explanation:
This video is classified as AI work because the young man and room appear realistic, but several fine details become less consistent as the shot changes. His fingers and the edges of the phone can look slightly unnatural when he picks it up, while the plastic takeaway bags, handles, and food containers do not remain completely stable. His blinking and facial expression are also very smooth and controlled. These clues are subtle because the lighting, framing, and face are otherwise convincing.

What to look for:
• Finger-to-phone contact appears slightly unnatural
• The phone edges may change subtly between frames
• Plastic-bag handles and folds are not fully consistent
• Blinking and facial movement look unusually smooth
• The scene is polished with very stable camera movement

Source: This video was generated by AI.

topic: A lone warrior confronting a samurai army
explanation:
This video is classified as AI work because it creates a convincing cinematic battlefield, but the large number of repeated soldiers produces visible synthetic patterns. Armour plates, helmet decorations, weapons, faces, and banner symbols change or lose detail across frames. Several background soldiers look very similar to one another, and the lone warrior's facial marks and armour texture are not perfectly stable. Fog and camera movement help conceal these inconsistencies, making the video moderately difficult.

What to look for:
• Several soldiers have repeated or very similar appearances
• Armour and helmet details shift between frames
• Banner symbols and weapon shapes are not fully stable
• Background faces lose detail or blend together
• Fog and cinematic camera movement hide the visual errors

Source: This video was generated by AI.

topic: A historical army preparing for battle on a beach
explanation:
This video is classified as human work because the actors, costumes, props, ships, shoreline, and crowds remain physically consistent across several edited shots. The bearded man's hand gestures and facial movements are natural, while the beach sequence contains believable waves, moving ships, soldiers, shadows, and atmospheric depth. The rapid changes between close-ups and wide shots still preserve the same historical setting and production design, which is typical of professionally filmed footage.

What to look for:
• Natural facial expressions and hand gestures
• Costumes and armour remain consistent across cuts
• Ships, waves, and shoreline follow realistic physics
• Large crowds maintain believable scale and depth
• Professional editing preserves scene continuity

Source: Real human work.

topic: Ancient wooden longships sailing at dusk
explanation:
This video is classified as human work because the wooden ships move through the sea with coherent weight, direction, and water resistance. The hulls, sails, rigging, oars, and people aboard remain stable as the camera follows them. Waves and wakes react naturally around the ships, while the changing light on the water and the low-light image texture resemble real cinematic photography. No major object-morphing or temporal instability is visible.

What to look for:
• Ship hulls, sails, and rigging remain stable
• Oars and crew movement are physically coherent
• Waves and wakes respond naturally to the ships
• Lighting changes realistically across the water
• Low-light grain and camera tracking look natural

Source: Real human work.

topic: A ceremonial martial-arts fight in a futuristic hall
explanation:
This video is classified as human work because the performers' bodies, clothing, weapons, and positions remain consistent throughout the ceremonial and combat sequences. The opening footstep interacts naturally with the sand, and later movements show realistic balance, momentum, falls, and weapon contact. The large hall keeps stable architecture, perspective, shadows, and lighting across changing camera angles. These details indicate coordinated live-action filming and professional visual production.

What to look for:
• The foot interacts naturally with the sand
• Clothing and weapons remain consistent during combat
• Body balance, momentum, and falls look realistic
• Architecture and perspective remain stable across cuts
• Lighting and shadows match the performers' movements

Source: Real human work

content: honestly idk why but today just felt off. woke up late, missed the bus, spilled coffee on my shirt right before the meeting lol. sometimes i wonder if the universe just has bad days planned for us or if its just me being clumsy as usual
topic: A frustrating and unlucky day
explanation:
This text is classified as AI work because it deliberately imitates casual human messaging through lowercase writing, abbreviations, missing punctuation, and 'lol.' It also includes a believable chain of small problems. The classification is difficult because these features look natural, but the sequence is unusually compact and complete, with several familiar mishaps arranged to establish one mood.

What to look for:
• Imitates casual lowercase messaging
• Uses abbreviations and slang
• Stacks several familiar mishaps
• Maintains one carefully constructed mood
• Could easily be mistaken for a real social post

Source: Generated by AI

content: My uncle Rudi used to fix motorcycles in his garage in Bandung. He'd let me hold the wrench sometimes, badly, and he never once got mad. Just laughed. Died in 2019. I still think about the smell of that garage — oil, dust, kretek smoke. Weird what sticks with you.
topic: Memories of an uncle's motorcycle garage
explanation:
This text is classified as AI work because it uses many features normally associated with authentic memoir: a named relative, a real city, a year, fragmented sentences, and sensory details. These details make the text intentionally difficult to detect. A possible clue is how efficiently every detail contributes to nostalgia and grief, producing a highly compressed, polished emotional arc without irrelevant or uncertain memories.

What to look for:
• Contains a name, location, and year
• Uses vivid smells and cultural detail
• Includes natural-looking sentence fragments
• Creates a complete emotional arc very efficiently
• Closely imitates autobiographical writing

Source: Generated by AI

content: Look, the idea that AI will "replace" writers is lazy thinking. What it'll actually do is expose which writers were never adding much value to begin with — the ones churning out generic listicles and SEO filler. Good writing survives because it comes from something the model doesn't have: actual stakes.
topic: The effect of AI on professional writers
explanation:
This text is classified as AI work because it convincingly imitates an opinionated columnist. It uses contractions, a direct opening, an em dash, industry language, and a strong final phrase. The subtle warning sign is that the argument is rhetorically sharp but simplified: it presents a confident binary between low-value and meaningful writing without evidence, exceptions, or a developed example.

What to look for:
• Uses a confident personal voice
• Includes industry terms such as SEO and listicles
• Ends with a memorable rhetorical phrase
• Presents a simplified either-or argument
• Offers no evidence or concrete case

Source: Generated by AI

content: The kitchen light was the only one still on. Dad had already gone to bed, but there was half a cup of tea on the table that had gone cold hours earlier. I remember touching the mug before washing it, as if that somehow delayed admitting the day was over.
topic: A quiet memory at the end of the day
explanation:
This text is classified as human work in the supplied dataset because it focuses on an ordinary physical action and leaves much of its emotional meaning unstated. The cold tea, the single light, and touching the mug create a subtle scene whose significance is implied rather than explained. This restraint and ambiguity make the passage feel like an observed personal moment, although authorship cannot be proven from style alone.

What to look for:
• Uses a small ordinary domestic scene
• Includes concrete objects and physical action
• Leaves emotion mostly unstated
• Allows ambiguity and reader interpretation
• The supplied note describes inspiration, not a direct source

Source: Written by human - Inspired by memoir style; similar narrative techniques found in Annie Ernaux's memoirs.

content: He always whistled the same tune before unlocking the shop. I couldn't tell you what song it was now, only that hearing anyone whistle something similar still makes me glance toward a door that isn't there anymore.
topic: Remembering someone through a familiar whistle
explanation:
This text is classified as human work in the supplied dataset because it connects memory to a small habitual sound and an involuntary present-day reaction. The writer openly admits not remembering the song, which introduces uncertainty rather than manufacturing complete detail. The final image of looking toward an absent door communicates loss indirectly and with emotional restraint.

What to look for:
• Builds memory around a specific habit
• Admits uncertainty about the song
• Links past experience to a present reaction
• Expresses loss indirectly
• The supplied note gives a stylistic comparison only

Source: Written by human f*rom the book: This Boy's Life by Tobias Wolff.

topic: First human landing on Mars
explanation:
This image is classified as AI work because it presents a highly realistic but fabricated breaking-news event. The astronaut, Martian landscape, flag, landing vehicle, and CNN-style graphics are visually consistent, so there are few obvious generation errors. However, the scene appears carefully constructed as a single dramatic composition, and the image provides no verifiable mission name, astronaut identity, date, or official source. The polished realism combined with an extraordinary unsupported claim makes it suitable for the hard level.

What to look for:
• Extraordinary claim without verifiable details
• Highly polished and cinematic composition
• News branding is imitated convincingly
• No mission name or astronaut identity is provided
• Few obvious visual defects make detection difficult

Source: This image was generated by AI

topic: Fabricated global hantavirus pandemic
explanation:
This image is classified as AI work because it imitates a professional health-emergency broadcast while presenting an extreme and unsupported claim. The anchor, laboratory image, infection map, ticker, and CNN-style layout appear realistic at first glance. The main warning signs are contextual: the claim of one billion deaths is sensational, the wording is awkward, and several organizations and cities are mentioned without precise reports, dates, experts, or evidence. The image is difficult because its visual quality is strong and the clues mainly come from the credibility and structure of the message.

What to look for:
• Extreme health claim presented without evidence
• Awkward headline wording
• Many authorities are mentioned without specific citations
• Realistic anchor and broadcast graphics
• Detection depends more on context than visual defects

Source: This image was generated by AI

topic: Legalization of gambling in an Arab country
explanation:
This image is classified as AI work because it combines a realistic television studio with an illustrated casino scene and a vague political headline. The broadcast design, presenter, map, and Arabic-English sign are convincing enough to resemble a real report. However, the story avoids naming the country, law, officials, date, or location, even though these details would normally be central to professional reporting. The generic wording and carefully staged illustration suggest that the image was synthetically created to look credible without providing verifiable information.

What to look for:
• The country is not identified
• No law, official, date, or location is named
• Inset casino scene looks illustrated and staged
• Broadcast layout closely imitates real television news
• Vague wording hides the lack of verifiable information

Source: This image was generated by AI

topic: United States investigation into Tesla Autopilot
explanation:
This image is classified as human work because it shows the structure of an authentic financial-news broadcast. The stock price, percentage change, one-year chart, company ticker, presenter window, CNBC logo, and investigation headline are arranged consistently. The image also contains natural video compression, realistic studio lighting, and ordinary imperfections that are typical of a captured television frame. Its information-rich layout appears to have been assembled through normal newsroom graphics and editorial production.

What to look for:
• Consistent CNBC branding and financial graphics
• Stock figures and chart follow a professional layout
• Natural presenter appearance and studio lighting
• Visible broadcast compression and ordinary imperfections
• Information appears organized by newsroom editors

Source: This is real news from CNBC television broadcast

topic: Television coverage of the Iran conflict
explanation:
This image is classified as human work because it appears to be a real capture of one broadcaster showing another broadcaster's coverage. The studio presenter, large background screen, Al Jazeera logo, news ticker, live time, and C-SPAN lower-third have different but internally consistent visual styles. The perspective, screen glare, cropping, text overlap, and compression are realistic consequences of recording or rebroadcasting television footage. These layered production details are more consistent with human broadcast workflows than with a single synthetic composition.

What to look for:
• Two different broadcast styles are layered consistently
• Realistic screen perspective and glare
• Natural cropping and text overlap
• Visible compression from rebroadcast footage
• Studio and lower-third graphics follow human production conventions

Source: This is real news from ALJAZEERA television broadcast

topic: Survival on a remote tropical beach
explanation:
This video is classified as AI work because it creates a convincing cinematic survival scene, but several details appear too controlled and may not remain fully consistent across time. The woman's hands, the food or tools she handles, strands of hair, clothing folds, and the nearby animal can subtly change as the shot progresses. The transition from the close shelter scene to the wide beach view also feels visually polished rather than naturally recorded. These small temporal continuity issues make the clip appropriate for the hard level because no single frame contains an obvious error.

What to look for:
• Hands and handled objects may change slightly between frames
• Hair and clothing details can shift during movement
• The nearby animal may show subtle shape or fur inconsistencies
• Lighting and composition look unusually cinematic
• The AI clues are mainly temporal rather than obvious in one frame

Source: Generated by AI.

topic: Mindfulness and awareness of breathing
explanation:
This video is classified as AI work because the meditating speaker looks realistic at first, yet the facial animation is unusually restrained and smooth. The lips, jaw, nostrils, eyelids, and skin texture may not move with the full complexity expected from natural speech and breathing. The robe edges and blurred grassland background also remain almost too stable while the camera changes from a distant view to a close-up. Because the face is highly realistic and the subtitles help the scene feel authentic, the synthetic clues are subtle.

What to look for:
• Lip and jaw movement may not fully match natural speech
• Blinking and breathing appear unusually controlled
• Skin texture remains very smooth during facial movement
• Robe edges and background stay almost too stable
• Realistic face and subtitles make the clip difficult to identify

Source: Generated by AI.

topic: Fabricated news reports about AI clones replacing humans
explanation:
This video is classified as AI work because it combines realistic news presenters, protest footage, crowds, captions, and channel graphics into a fabricated report about AI clones. The rapid editing makes the video persuasive, but closer inspection reveals inconsistent station branding, awkward lower-third text, unstable protest-sign lettering, and crowd faces or hands that may change between frames. The sensational story is supported by many visually convincing shots, so viewers must examine both the content and temporal details rather than relying on one obvious mistake.

What to look for:
• News-channel names and branding are inconsistent
• Some lower-third text is awkward or incomplete
• Protest-sign lettering may shift between shots
• Crowd faces and hands can contain temporal distortions
• Fast editing hides many of the synthetic inconsistencies

Source: Generated by AI.

topic: Wildlife documentary about Pallas's cats in a snowy habitat
explanation:
This video is classified as human work because it contains extended wildlife footage with complex, unpredictable animal behaviour. The cats' fur, anatomy, footprints, shadows, and movement remain coherent across different locations and camera angles. Snow, grass, rocks, wind, depth of field, and interactions with other animals follow natural physical patterns. The long duration and changing documentary shots also preserve consistent environmental detail that would be difficult to synthesize without visible drift.

What to look for:
• Animal anatomy and fur remain consistent over a long sequence
• Footprints and movement interact naturally with snow and ground
• Wildlife behaviour is varied and unpredictable
• Focus, camera distance, and environmental conditions change naturally
• The habitat remains physically coherent across many shots

Source: Real - the video was taken by human.

topic: An outdoor chess game and conversation
explanation:
This video is classified as human work because it shows a naturally filmed conversation between two people playing chess. Their faces, hairstyles, clothing, posture, and positions around the table remain consistent as the camera changes between close-ups and reverse angles. The chess pieces, board, hands, background pedestrians, trees, and buildings follow normal perspective and depth. Small facial reactions, eye movements, pauses, and focus changes are also consistent with live-action cinematography.

What to look for:
• Faces, clothing, and hairstyles remain consistent across cuts
• Natural eye movement and subtle facial reactions
• Chess pieces and board follow realistic perspective
• Shot-reverse-shot editing preserves spatial continuity
• Background activity and depth of field behave naturally

Source: Real - the video was taken by human

topic: A reflective visit to a historical garden or monument
explanation:
This video is classified as human work because the person, monument, courtyard, and trees remain stable throughout the camera movement. The footage contains realistic exposure changes, framing imperfections, motion blur, natural tree movement, and ordinary camera shake. The distant person moves with believable scale and perspective, while the architecture retains fine detail across the wide shot. The subtitle 'Live a little.' is consistently overlaid like normal edited footage rather than being generated as part of the environment.

What to look for:
• Architecture and environmental details remain stable
• Natural camera shake and framing imperfections
• Realistic motion blur and exposure changes
• The person maintains correct scale and perspective
• Subtitle placement remains consistent as an edited overlay

Source: Real - the video was taken by human.
